description: Goal: make compiler2 macros compile-time-only facts. A defmacro should be published, defined, lowered to an interpreter-ready backend artifact, used to expand quoted AST, and then stop; it must not seed a semantic/runtime root or enter materialize/abi/emission/native pipelines. Signal: item/local/required macro expansion waits only on compile-time macro readiness; macro definitions no longer churn FunctionSource/FunctionDefined via BackendProgram->MacroExecutable feedback; telemetry proves defmacro jobs never enter runtime root/materialization/codegen loops. Strategy: first produce the interpreter-ready macro backend directly from defined macro bodies, then rewire source expansion to depend on that fact alone, then delete macro-root/artifact scaffolding and restate the invariant in tests/docs.
title: Compiler2 macro pipeline: stop defmacro at interpreter-ready backend
